channel.title: Book-Reviews on Tedsig42
channel.description: Recent content in Book-Reviews on Tedsig42

item.title: Why Book Review ?
item.description:
In this section, I share reviews of books I use to learn and improve in infosec.

There will be two review formats:

  • Some books will be covered through multiple chapters/posts to go deeper step by step.
  • Others will be treated in a single post with a fast global summary.

Most of the books come from publishers like No Starch Press, O’Reilly, or Packt Publishing.

I personally started infosec by reading Phrack and solving Root-Me challenges, and I believe books are still one of the best ways to build strong fundamentals.

item.title: x86-64 Assembly Language Programming with Ubuntu written by Ed Jorgensen
item.description:
I am starting with the book x86-64 Assembly Language Programming with Ubuntu , written by Dr. Ed Jorgensen.
I use mdBook to take notes and make diagrams, often with Excalidraw.
Why? Because I want to learn it, and it can be useful in the future.

The book is published under a Creative Commons license, so you can get it here.

My work can be found here

item.title: From Day 0 to 0day Chapter 2
item.description:
MAPPING CODE TO ATTACK SURFACE

Once we know where we are, then the world becomes as narrow as a map.
When we don’t know, the world feels unlimited. Liu Cixin, The Dark Forest

Introduction

Attack surface (the potential entry points to exploit a vulnerability) often increases with the growing complexity of software.
The vulnerabilities to be introduced, as developers’ capacity to properly secure these feature is limited and mistakes are inevitable when dealing with millions of lines of code.
Minor issues can be chained together into far more serious vulnerabilities.

item.title: From Day 0 to 0day Chapter 1
item.description:
Introduction

Taint Analysis

Life is not like water. Things in life don’t necessarily flow over the shortest possible route - Haruki Murakami - 1Q84

Taint analysis (or source and sink analysis ) is the analysis of the flow of input through a program from sources to sinks .

It relies on a simple idea: a large number of vulnerabilities occur because attacker controlled input (the source) flows to a dangerous function (the sink). If the input modifies other variables along the way, these variables become “tainted” and are included in the analysis.

item.title: From Day 0 to 0day Chapter 0
item.description:
This is my summary and review of the book From Day Zero to Zero Day by Eugene “Spaceraccoon” Lim, a security researcher and white-hat hacker.

He learned rapidly because his first training was in how to learn. - Frank Herbert, Dune.

With the number of discovered and exploited zero days constantly growing, vulnerability research, or the process of analyzing systems for new vulnerabilities, has assumed a critical role in cybersecurity.
